recommended_survey.instructions: Select the five tasks that matter most to you when visiting this HIPAA Privacy Rule section. Then rank those five tasks from most important to least important. Use plain-language task statements only. Randomize order for each participant.
task_list_for_voting: Determine if HIPAA applies to me
Find guidance on public health disclosures
Review HIPAA rule updates and history
Find notice of privacy practices requirements
Understand state law interactions with HIPAA

next_steps: Validate top tasks through quantitative task voting research
Analyze onsite search logs to compare inferred versus actual user intent
Map top tasks to current navigation and identify friction points
Conduct usability testing on top five tasks
Evaluate whether regulatory and legal content is too dense for general public audiences
Assess cross-linking between Privacy Rule, Security Rule, FAQs, and complaint pathways
Prioritize homepage and navigation changes that elevate validated top tasks
Measure completion success rates for compliance-oriented journeys
summary: The dominant user needs center on understanding HIPAA coverage, protected information, permitted disclosures, and core privacy rule requirements. Compliance-oriented tasks clearly outweigh archival or policy-history tasks. The strongest top tasks are practical and high-risk: determining whether HIPAA applies, understanding PHI handling rules, and accessing authoritative regulation text. FAQ access also appears highly valuable because users likely seek quick clarification over deep legal reading. Key risks include dense legal language, fragmented navigation between Privacy and Security Rules, and moderate discoverability for nuanced compliance topics like minimum necessary standards and incidental disclosures. Validation should focus on whether professionals and the public prioritize the same tasks, how users phrase these intents in plain language, and where users struggle to complete compliance-related journeys.
